title: OAuth, Secrets, Identity, and Least Privilege · Design Defense
summary: Secure a remote MCP server serving user-specific data. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an OAuth and credential-handling design. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Decide when authentication and user authorization are required by data and action scope.
Implement protected resource metadata and OAuth-aligned authorization for remote HTTP.
Validate token audience, issuer, scopes, expiry, and resource binding.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Least Privilege Tools applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Map scopes and approvals to narrow tools and user intent.
